# Chronicles Index — Auto-Generated

**No manual index management is needed.**

The chronicles index at `/chronicles` is automatically generated by Next.js from the MDX
files in `content/blog/`. The page reads all `.mdx` files, parses their frontmatter
(`title`, `date`, `rune`, `excerpt`, `slug`), and renders a responsive card grid sorted
by date (newest first).

## How it works

- **Index page:** `development/frontend/src/app/(marketing)/chronicles/page.tsx`
  - Uses `getAllChronicles()` from `@/lib/chronicles` to list all entries
- **Detail page:** `development/frontend/src/app/(marketing)/chronicles/[slug]/page.tsx`
  - Uses `getChronicleBySlug(slug)` to render individual chronicles via MDXRemote
- **Data source:** `development/frontend/content/blog/*.mdx`
  - Parsed by `development/frontend/src/lib/chronicles.ts` using `gray-matter`

## Adding a new chronicle

Simply write the MDX file to `content/blog/{{NAME}}.mdx` using `generate-chronicle.ts`.
The index and detail pages will pick it up automatically on the next build.

The chronicle will be available at `/chronicles/{{NAME}}`.
